verses.text: 'How amiable are thy tabernacles, O LORD of hosts!' the psalmist cries; his soul longs and faints for the courts of God. He aches to be where God is worshipped. The believer's heart is drawn to the place of God's presence.

'How amiable are thy tabernacles, O LORD of hosts!' the psalmist cries; his soul longs and faints for the courts of God. He aches to be where God is worshipped. The believer's heart is drawn to the place of God's presence.
Even the sparrow finds a home near God's altars; 'blessed are they that dwell in thy house.' He envies the birds their nearness. Happiest are those who abide continually in fellowship with God.
Even the sparrow finds a home near God's altars; 'blessed are they that dwell in thy house.' He envies the birds their nearness. Happiest are those who abide continually in fellowship with God.

Blessed is the one whose strength is in God, who passing through the valley of weeping makes it a well, going 'from strength to strength.' The pilgrim grows on the way. Each stage of the journey to God renews, not drains, the traveller's strength.

Blessed is the one whose strength is in God, who passing through the valley of weeping makes it a well, going 'from strength to strength.' The pilgrim grows on the way. Each stage of the journey to God renews, not drains, the traveller's strength.

Blessed is the one whose strength is in God, who passing through the valley of weeping makes it a well, going 'from strength to strength.' The pilgrim grows on the way. Each stage of the journey to God renews, not drains, the traveller's strength.
He prays God to hear and to look upon His anointed, the people's shield. He commits the king and cause to God. The welfare of God's anointed is the welfare of His people.
He prays God to hear and to look upon His anointed, the people's shield. He commits the king and cause to God. The welfare of God's anointed is the welfare of His people.

'A day in thy courts is better than a thousand,' he says; he would rather be a doorkeeper in God's house than dwell with the wicked, for God withholds 'no good thing' from the upright. Nearness to God outweighs every earthly pleasure. He is sun and shield to all who walk before Him.
